data.title: zSpace, Inc. Faces Nasdaq Non-Compliance Notice
data.description:
The latest update is out from zSpace, Inc. ( (ZSPC) ).

On October 1, 2025, zSpace, Inc. received a notice from Nasdaq indicating non-compliance with the requirement to maintain a minimum market value of publicly held shares of at least $15 million. The company has 180 days to regain compliance, failing which it may face delisting. zSpace, Inc. plans to monitor its market value and explore options to meet Nasdaq’s criteria, including potentially transferring to the Nasdaq Capital Market.

The most recent analyst rating on (ZSPC) stock is a Sell with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on zSpace, Inc. stock, see the ZSPC Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Orion Energy Systems Finalizes Acquisition Agreements
data.description:
Orion Energy Systems ( (OESX) ) has shared an update.

On September 30, 2025, Orion Energy Systems finalized several financial and operational agreements related to its acquisition of Voltrek, LLC. The company settled its fiscal 2024 earnout obligations with payments totaling $875,000 and issued $1 million in common stock to Kathleen Connors for fiscal 2025 obligations. A subordinated loan agreement was established with Final Frontier to defer remaining earnout payments, with monthly principal and interest payments scheduled through July 2027. Additionally, a security agreement was made to secure obligations, subordinated to Bank of America’s senior credit facilities. The company also entered into a support agreement with Final Frontier and Connors, restricting their influence on company affairs, and granted Connors a non-voting board observer role.

data.title: Occidental Sells OxyChem to Berkshire Hathaway
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum ( (OXY) ) has issued an announcement.

On October 1, 2025, Occidental Petroleum‘s subsidiaries, Occidental Chemical Holding, LLC and Environmental Resource Holdings, LLC, entered into an agreement to sell Occidental Chemical Corporation (OxyChem) to Berkshire Hathaway for $9.7 billion in an all-cash transaction. The deal, which includes customary adjustments and conditions, will see Berkshire Hathaway acquire all equity interests in OxyChem, with Occidental retaining certain environmental liabilities.
